observations: The CRM outcome is a model interpretation over relationship evidence, not a sales score, threshold, or deterministic referral preference.
Prospect and Outreach learning should become approved doctrine with source refs before downstream apps change behavior.
The referral path matters because the model interprets the outcome in context; code should not hardcode referral weighting or tone rules.

state_proposals.interpretation: Referral-path evidence may improve prospecting context when relationship outcome proves implementation relevance.
uncertainty: One outcome may not be enough to generalize broadly.
Human owner has not approved doctrine promotion.

state_proposals.interpretation: Referral-originated opportunities may require follow-up that preserves relationship context rather than generic sequence tone.
uncertainty: The right outreach language should remain model-mediated and context-specific.

action_proposals.summary: Create Prospect Researcher referral doctrine candidate.
Create Outreach Engine referral follow-up doctrine candidate.

rollup_requests.reason: Doctrine rollup should refresh after approval or rejection.
Doctrine rollup should refresh after approval or rejection.

uncertainty: Approval is required before any app changes behavior.

missing_evidence.summary: Human approval for Prospect Researcher doctrine update
Human approval for Outreach Engine doctrine update

review_signal.summary: CRM outcome produced Prospect and Outreach doctrine candidates; both remain approval gated.
